traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Audhild, age 10)

q01.name: The Restless Crypt
q01.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q01.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Aldric, age 8)

q02.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q02.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q02.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Tomas, age 8)

q03.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q03.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q03.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q04.name: The Giant of the Border Marches
q04.desc: Border forts flattened, garrisons walking home weaponless. The marshal wants the marches quiet again.
q04.epilogue: The forts are re-garrisoned; the marches go quiet, and stay so.

q05.name: The Renegade Magus
q05.desc: A court wizard who stopped asking leave. The crown pays for the tower dark and the fires out.
q05.epilogue: The tower stands dark and cold; the crown's sage carries the renegade's books away under seal.

q06.name: The Ash-Callers' Rebellion
q06.desc: Shamans who traded the clan's fire for their own. The true shaman pays iron for them silenced.
q06.epilogue: The circle's ashes are scattered on running water; the clan's fire burns in one pit again, where it belongs.

q07.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q07.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q07.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q08.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q08.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q08.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q09.name: Rival Warband
q09.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q09.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q10.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q10.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q10.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q11.name: The Boiler-Cult
q11.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q11.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

q12.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q12.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q12.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

q13.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q13.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q13.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q14.name: The Grudge War
q14.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.
q14.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q15.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q15.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.
q15.epilogue: The seams ring with picks again by week's end.

q16.name: The Rune-Fire Below
q16.desc: Chanters feeding forbidden runes in a sealed gallery. The runesmiths want the fire out before the stone learns it.
q16.epilogue: The vault is re-sealed under three locks and a new rune; the warden files the party's names under 'safe hands'.

q17.name: The Flooded Hold
q17.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.
q17.epilogue: The heir walks the dry galleries once, dry-eyed, and seals the hold behind -- some doors are better shut.

q18.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q18.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q18.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q19.name: The Ash-Callers' Rebellion
q19.desc: Shamans who traded the clan's fire for their own. The true shaman pays iron for them silenced.
q19.epilogue: The circle's ashes are scattered on running water; the clan's fire burns in one pit again, where it belongs.

q20.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q20.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q20.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q21.name: The Hedge-Wizards' Toll
q21.desc: Renegade hedge-wizards tax the road in fire and frost. The witchfinder pays for the tollhouse dark.
q21.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

q22.name: The Hedge-Wizards' Toll
q22.desc: Renegade hedge-wizards tax the road in fire and frost. The witchfinder pays for the tollhouse dark.
q22.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

q23.name: Rival Warband
q23.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q23.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q24.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q24.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q24.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

q25.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q25.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q25.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q26.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q26.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q26.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q27.name: The Proving Hunt
q27.desc: The clan sets a beast worth a name. Bring back its hide and the clan pays in iron and standing.
q27.epilogue: The hide hangs in the long-tent and the party's name is spoken at the fire -- iron and standing, paid in full.

q28.name: The Restless Crypt
q28.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q28.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q29.name: Wolves at the Folds
q29.desc: Sheep gone, then a shepherd. The commons want the pack dug out of the hills.
q29.epilogue: Lambing season passes without a beast lost; the elder salts the den's earth as the old rites ask.

q30.name: Things in the Deep Roads
q30.desc: The under-way to the second hold is silked and skittering. The toll-guild pays by the cleared mile.
q30.epilogue: The under-way reopens by the cleared mile, tolls half-price for a season in the party's honor.

q31.name: The Ash-Callers' Rebellion
q31.desc: Shamans who traded the clan's fire for their own. The true shaman pays iron for them silenced.
q31.epilogue: The circle's ashes are scattered on running water; the clan's fire burns in one pit again, where it belongs.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Dench, a orc rival adventurer, level 6, out there somewhere)

q32.name: Things in the Deep Roads
q32.desc: The under-way to the second hold is silked and skittering. The toll-guild pays by the cleared mile.
q32.epilogue: The under-way reopens by the cleared mile, tolls half-price for a season in the party's honor.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Dain, age 9)

q33.name: The Giant of the Border Marches
q33.desc: Border forts flattened, garrisons walking home weaponless. The marshal wants the marches quiet again.
q33.epilogue: The forts are re-garrisoned; the marches go quiet, and stay so.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Blatz, a goblin bounty hunter, level 9, out there somewhere)

q34.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q34.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q34.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Inga, a human mercenary captain, level 3, out there somewhere)

q35.name: The Restless Crypt
q35.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q35.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q36.name: The Proving Hunt
q36.desc: The clan sets a beast worth a name. Bring back its hide and the clan pays in iron and standing.
q36.epilogue: The hide hangs in the long-tent and the party's name is spoken at the fire -- iron and standing, paid in full.

q37.name: The Grudge War
q37.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.
q37.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q38.name: Ashes Going Home
q38.desc: One of theirs died far from home. The family wants the ashes carried the whole way, and carried right.
q38.epilogue: The urn goes into the ground beside its people; the kin set a place at the table for the party that night.

q39.name: The Prisoner's Ransom
q39.desc: A magistrate buys a hostage back from across the border. The gold must arrive uncounted and unopened.
q39.epilogue: The hostage walks free at the next new moon; the broker, professionally offended by honesty, pays out to the coin.

q41.name: The Debt Collection
q41.desc: A whole village signed, sealed, and stopped paying. The moneylender pays a fifth of the book to whoever squeezes it out of them -- and the village has raised a militia.
q41.epilogue: The village pays to the copper, then names its new well after the party. The bucket, specifically.

q44.name: Dine and Dash
q44.desc: Take the best table, order everything, compliment the cellar -- then rob the owner to settle the bill. The house employs discreet, well-fed muscle.

deed.text: the long con -- twelve courses of charm, ending alone with the host and the strongbox
deed.fail: the sommelier remembers your face from a poster

q44.epilogue: The review, dictated to a terrified waiter on the way out: five stars, would rob again.

q43.name: The Five-Finger Discount
q43.desc: A collector wants one small, priceless thing lifted from a merchant's strongroom. Then there is the fence, who has opinions about prices.

deed.text: the lift -- in through the coal chute, out with the goods, touching nothing
deed.fail: a shelf goes over; the whole house wakes

twist.text: The fence turns the piece over once and offers HALF its price -- 'damaged in transit, see' -- and his bodyguards lean off the wall in unison.
twist.accept: Half is half. The fence smiles like a purse closing.

q43.epilogue: The piece surfaces in a private gallery three lands away. The merchant posts a reward; the Watch posts a description; neither is close.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Jenna, a human bounty hunter, level 6, out there somewhere)

w1.name: Beautiful Machines at the Border
w1.desc: Surveyors in gilded coats mark farms they do not own in the orc borderlands; the machines that follow them do not ask twice.
w1.epilogue: The surveyors withdraw with perfect courtesy, leaving pegs, a burned camp, and a promise: this was a measurement, not a defeat.

q46.name: The Vault Job
q46.desc: The counting-house vault holds three lands' worth of deposits. The plan is elegant. The guards are not part of the plan.

deed.text: the plan -- cased for a week, timed to the minute, in through the lamplit gallery between rounds
deed.fail: the rounds changed this morning; the plan meets the guards

q46.epilogue: Three counting-houses fail by month's end and the broker, who sold their debts short, sends a fruit basket. It is not poisoned. Probably.

q45.name: The Debt Collection
q45.desc: A whole village signed, sealed, and stopped paying. The moneylender pays a fifth of the book to whoever squeezes it out of them -- and the village has raised a militia.
q45.epilogue: The village pays to the copper, then names its new well after the party. The bucket, specifically.

q47.name: The Last Sermon
q47.desc: Hell wants the local priest on the altar he preaches from. He is, for the record, a bad man -- a usurer in vestments who has ruined more families than any bandit. Will you still chicken out?
q47.epilogue: The parish, freed of its usurer, is oddly quiet about the how. The ledger of debts burns in the vestry grate, and nobody claims to have lit it.

q48.name: Dine and Dash
q48.desc: Take the best table, order everything, compliment the cellar -- then rob the owner to settle the bill. The house employs discreet, well-fed muscle.

deed.text: the long con -- twelve courses of charm, ending alone with the host and the strongbox
deed.fail: the sommelier remembers your face from a poster

q48.epilogue: The review, dictated to a terrified waiter on the way out: five stars, would rob again.
